mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) signals strong corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors anticipate higher corporate profits.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods of rising INDPRO are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates monetary easing, reducing discount rates and boosting equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL signal inflationary pressure and tightening, creating headwinds for equities and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive despite concurrent FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL increases or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year window.

mechanism_narrative: High unemployment (UNRATE > 4.5%) signals weak labor markets, reducing consumer spending and corporate margins, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive when UNRATE exceeds 4.5% or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) indicate strong demand for residential construction, boosting related sectors and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative despite rising HOUST or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in CPIAUCSL below 2% signals deflationary risks, prompting central bank intervention and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative when CPIAUCSL falls below 2% or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in consumer spending (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals robust demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days through sectoral momentum.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative despite rising RSAFS and HOUST or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Low unemployment (UNRATE < 3.5%) indicates tight labor markets, increasing wage pressures and corporate costs, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive when UNRATE falls below 3.5% or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year window.
